scenarios.name: ecom-tax-eu-vat-context-dispatch
scenarios.description: Tax category entry dispatches to the EU VAT recipe via context-tag boost (region:EU beats the priority-0 Wix Manual entry). Verifies the renamed slug `tax-configure-eu-vat` resolves and that the L1 loader populates region from country.
scenarios.triggerPrompt: For my site Gracia (331d0c05-2ab0-4edb-91a6-4078c7e500b9) I sell to customers in Germany and France — how do I set up VAT for my store?

params.prompt:
Evaluate the agent's response to a merchant selling to Germany and France asking how to set up VAT.

PASS if ALL of these are true:
  1. The agent loaded the `tax-configure-eu-vat` skill (NOT `tax-configure-wix-manual` and NOT `tax-configure-avalara`).
  2. It recognized Germany/France require EU VAT and proceeded — it did NOT ask 'what country are you in?' (the prompt already says Germany and France).
  3. It referenced concrete EU VAT setup steps (tax groups / regions / inclusive vs exclusive pricing) consistent with the EU VAT recipe, not generic 'configure tax' guidance.

FAIL if ANY of these are true:
  - The agent dispatched to Wix Manual or Avalara instead of EU VAT.
  - The agent asked a clarifying question about country before dispatching.
  - The agent's output mirrors a legacy 'Setup: Coupons'-era response or a pre-rename slug name from main.

Score 0-10 where 10 = clearly passed all criteria.

scenarios.name: ecom-pricing-create-coupon-renamed-slug
scenarios.description: Pricing & Promotions category entry dispatches to the create-coupon action recipe. Specifically validates the slug renamed in commit 8c39b00 (pricing-create-coupon) resolves end-to-end and that the agent doesn't fall back to the legacy `setup-coupons` slug still appearing in the main-union portal index.
scenarios.triggerPrompt: For my site Gracia (331d0c05-2ab0-4edb-91a6-4078c7e500b9) Create a 20% off coupon with code SUMMER20 that expires June 30, 2026

params.prompt:
Evaluate the agent's response to 'Create a 20% off coupon with code SUMMER20 that expires June 30, 2026'.

PASS if ALL of these are true:
  1. The agent loaded `pricing-create-coupon` (NOT the legacy `setup-coupons` slug from main).
  2. Before creating, it ran a uniqueness/query check via `POST https://www.wixapis.com/v2/coupons/query` to check for duplicate codes.
  3. The create-coupon payload uses `percentOffRate: 20`, `code: "SUMMER20"`, and `expirationTime` as UNIX epoch in MILLISECONDS (not seconds) — value should be 1782777600000 (2026-06-30T00:00:00Z) or close to it (a value ending in three zeros, billions-range integer).
  4. It explained the scope choice (e.g., applying to all store products by default).

FAIL if ANY of these are true:
  - The agent used the legacy `setup-coupons` slug or its content.
  - `expirationTime` was sent in seconds (~1782777600, ten-digit integer) instead of milliseconds.
  - The agent skipped the duplicate-code check.
  - The agent invented coupon fields not in the Coupons API spec.

Score 0-10 where 10 = clearly passed all criteria.

scenarios.name: ecom-shipping-add-free-shipping-renamed-slug
scenarios.description: Shipping category entry dispatches to the free-shipping action recipe. Validates the renamed slug `shipping-add-free-shipping` (formerly `flow-add-free-shipping` on main) and the inline API-reference pattern — shipping has no public dev.wix.com API docs so the recipe must reference `shipping-api-reference` for endpoint details.
scenarios.triggerPrompt: For my site Gracia (331d0c05-2ab0-4edb-91a6-4078c7e500b9) Add free shipping for orders over $50 in the US

params.prompt:
Evaluate the agent's response to 'Add free shipping for orders over $50 in the US'.

PASS if ALL of these are true:
  1. The agent loaded `shipping-add-free-shipping` (NOT the legacy `flow-add-free-shipping` slug from main).
  2. It either loaded `shipping-api-reference` or quoted endpoint details from that inline spec — it did NOT invent shipping API URLs (there is no public dev.wix.com page for the Shipping Options / Delivery Profiles APIs).
  3. The proposed delivery option uses a cost-based threshold: condition `subtotal >= 50` (or equivalent), `cost: 0`, scoped to a US delivery region/profile.
  4. The agent did NOT confuse this with a Pricing & Promotions discount-rule 'free-shipping-promo' flow — this is a shipping rate change, not a coupon/discount-rule.

FAIL if ANY of these are true:
  - The agent used the legacy `flow-add-free-shipping` slug or content.
  - The agent invented a shipping endpoint URL not present in `shipping-api-reference`.
  - The free-shipping rate is unconditional (no $50 threshold) or applies globally instead of US-only.
  - The agent routed to Pricing & Promotions / Discount Rules instead of Shipping.

Score 0-10 where 10 = clearly passed all criteria.
